rulesets.rule_note: Core Battle Rules
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Levels and IVs no longer exist.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Pokémon stats are standardized to level 50 with perfect IV equivalent values.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.category: • EVs are replaced by SP (1 SP = 1 stat point).
rulesets.rule_note: • EVs are replaced by SP (1 SP = 1 stat point).
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Pokémon can gain one additional stat point compared to previous EV systems.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • There is no 6v6 battling in Pokémon Champions, including private games.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: Battle Formats
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Singles uses a bring 6, pick 3 format with one Pokémon active at a time.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Doubles uses a bring 6, pick 4 format with two Pokémon active at a time.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Switching is generally safer in Singles due to fewer active threats.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Doubles switching is riskier due to multiple targets and higher damage output.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Support moves such as Tailwind, Helping Hand, Fake Out, and Protect are significantly more common in Doubles.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.

rulesets.rule_note: • Singles battles tend to last longer due to more frequent switching and positioning.
rulesets.source: Workbook rules tab / Pokémon Champions official rules screenshots supplied by user
rulesets.confidenceReason: Existing local data preserved; official Pokémon Champions confirmation still required before treating as verified.
